title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes makes use of a number of certificates as part of its operation. You should set the ownership of the directory containing the PKI information and all files in that directory to maintain their integrity. The directory and files should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, ls -laR /etc/kubernetes/pki/ Verify that the ownership of all files and directories in this hierarchy is set to root:root .
input_row.remediation: Run the below command (based on the file location on your system) on the Control Plane node. For example, chown -R root:root /etc/kubernetes/pki/
input_row.default_value: By default, the /etc/kubernetes/pki/ directory and all of the files and directories contained within it, are set to be owned by the root user.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit mentions running a command on the Control Plane node to check file ownership, which implies a need for access to the filesystem. However, since the control is about ensuring the ownership of the Kubernetes PKI directory and files, this can be verified through the Kubernetes API or by using `kubectl` commands to check the configuration of the control plane components.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of maintaining the integrity of the PKI files, which is a standard Kubernetes service property.
- The ownership of files and directories is a configuration aspect that can be checked programmatically, as it relates to the security posture of the Kubernetes cluster.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pki_directory_file_ownership_root

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use `kubectl` to check the configuration of the Kubernetes API server and kubelet, which may reference the PKI directory.
- The automated program would check the ownership of the files in the `/etc/kubernetes/pki/` directory.
- The specific command to verify the ownership would be something like:
  ```bash
  kubectl exec -n kube-system <api-server-pod-name> -- ls -la /etc/kubernetes/pki/
  ```
- The expected configuration would be that all files and directories in `/etc/kubernetes/pki/` are owned by `root:root`.
- Pass criteria: All files and directories should show ownership as `root:root`. If any file or directory is owned by a different user or group, it would fail the check.
